title: From Approval to Access: governed authoritative product-identity amendment
researchQuestion: Can information available at a drug-state-quarter observation predict next-quarter generic entry and, under stated observational assumptions, what is the estimated ATT risk difference of high lagged eligible-neighbor adoption exposure on next-quarter entry?
population: Eligible FDA first-generic approval proxy x scientifically eligible state x observable Medicaid quarter
drugIdentityPolicy: authoritative-product-family-v1.1: GenericLaunchId is a strength-specific FDA reference-product family keyed by normalized application number, product number, exact active-ingredient set, dosage form, route, and strength; RLD, reference-standard, and therapeutic-equivalence relationships are retained as evidence; conflicting or non-unique candidates remain unresolved
stateEligibilityPolicy: state-eligibility-v1; eligibility is versioned and distinct from data completeness
territoryPolicy: territories are not rejected by name; eligibility follows the versioned state/territory reference policy
entryDefinition: any-positive-v1: first eligible quarter with observed positive utilization; missing and suppressed are not zero

distributionDefinitions.weightedDistribution: 100 * frozen pre-outcome baseline weight among active states / total eligible baseline weight

predictive.observationTime: end of observation quarter using only information available by that cutoff
predictive.split: TemporalGroupedHoldout by FDA approval date with whole GenericLaunchId groups: training 2021-01-01 through 2022-12-31; validation 2023-01-01 through 2023-12-31; locked test 2024-01-01 through 2024-12-31; no GenericLaunchId overlap among partitions
predictive.testPolicy: 2024-01-01 through 2024-12-31 FDA approval cohorts with complete outcome-specific follow-up; locked from selection and evaluated once after model selection

causal.treatment: HighNeighborAdoptionExposure; prior-quarter eligible-neighbor adoption share; threshold 0.50; minimum eligible-peer count 2; lag 1 quarter; neighbor-v1; the primary minimum cannot change based on outcome results
causal.timeZero: observation-quarter start; baseline covariates available no later than treatment measurement
causal.adjustmentSet: peer-exposure-adjustment-v1; pretreatment leakage-safe declared confounders only
causal.positivity: empirical common support; propensity clipping [0.02,0.98] is sensitivity-only; report effective sample size and extreme weights

authoritativeIdentitySources: FDA Orange Book frozen downloadable files
FDA NDC Directory or complete openFDA NDC snapshot for listing/application linkage only
frozen RxNorm full monthly release including current and historical NDC associations
mappingHierarchy: Tier A: exact normalized Medicaid NDC to FDA listing application, then exact application/product to Orange Book or Drugs@FDA approval evidence
Tier B: exact normalized NDC to RxCUI, then exact normalized clinical drug or ingredient/form/strength to exactly one FDA launch family
Tier C: exact ingredient set, dosage form, route, and strength to exactly one valid launch family

mappingRules.repackagersRelabelers: link only through exact original-packager/application evidence; never manufacturer-name similarity
mappingRules.multiplePackages: many package NDCs may map to one exact strength-specific FDA product; preserve every package edge
mappingRules.combinationDrugs: compare normalized ingredient sets exactly; order-insensitive but multiplicity preserving
mappingRules.multipleStrengths: separate GenericLaunchId unless a future approved amendment justifies a strength-family estimand
mappingRules.discontinuedNdc: retain dated historical linkage and status; do not infer contemporaneous marketing
mappingRules.evidenceRetention: store snapshot hash, source record key, rule tier/version, method, confidence class, candidates, decision and UTC audit

dataPolicies.missing: explicit missingness; training-only fitted replacement where applicable
dataPolicies.suppression: unknown-not-zero; prescription and reimbursement remain null
dataPolicies.ambiguousNdc: preserve all four affected records as Ambiguous/Unresolved and exclude from normalized analysis
dataPolicies.invalidNdc: preserve both affected records as Invalid/Unresolved and exclude from normalized analysis
dataPolicies.ndcMappingChange: mapping changes after approval require a documented amendment or new protocol version
dataPolicies.duplicates: canonical source identities cannot be imported twice
dataPolicies.censoring: only observations with outcome-specific required follow-up are included; incomplete outcomes remain censored/null

sensitivityAnalyses: eligible-peer minimum counts 1 and 3 while retaining 2 as primary without outcome-based change
leakagePrevention: whole GenericLaunchId groups remain in one chronological partition
historical market-weight window ends before outcome
protocolDeviationPolicy: No outcome-driven change. Record deviations with actor, UTC time, reason and impact; material changes require a new protocol version and approval.
humanApproval: Draft must be submitted to UnderReview and approved by a named human. Approval does not approve acquisition, import, mapping, finalization, training, or causal estimation.
